recommendations.title: The Tenant
recommendations.overview: A quiet and inconspicuous man rents an apartment in Paris where he finds himself drawn into a rabbit hole of dangerous paranoia.

recommendations.title: Death and the Maiden
recommendations.overview: A political activist is convinced that her guest is a man who once tortured her for the government.

recommendations.title: Bitter Moon
recommendations.overview: A passenger on a cruise ship develops an irresistible infatuation with an eccentric paraplegic's wife.

recommendations.title: Venus in Fur
recommendations.overview: An enigmatic actress may have a hidden agenda when she auditions for a part in a misogynistic writer's play.

recommendations.title: Repulsion
recommendations.overview: Beautiful young manicurist Carole suffers from androphobia (the pathological fear of interaction with men). When her sister and roommate, Helen, leaves their London flat to go on an Italian holiday with her married boyfriend, Carole withdraws into he

recommendations.title: Pirates
recommendations.overview: The adventures of pirate Captain Red and his first mate Frog.

recommendations.title: Cul-de-sac
recommendations.overview: On the run and in search of help, two wounded gangsters find refuge in the secluded castle of a feeble man and his wife; however, under the point of a gun, nothing is what it seems.

recommendations.title: What?
recommendations.overview: A young American woman traveling through Italy finds herself in a strange Mediterranean villa where nothing seems quite right.

recommendations.title: The Pianist
recommendations.overview: The true story of pianist Władysław Szpilman's experiences in Warsaw during the Nazi occupation. When the Jews of the city find themselves forced into a ghetto, Szpilman finds work playing in a café; and when his family is deported in 1942, he stays

recommendations.title: Carnage
recommendations.overview: Two pairs of parents hold a cordial meeting after their sons are involved in a fight, though as their time together progresses, increasingly childish behavior throws the discussion into chaos.
